description: Deterministic test quality rules — consumed by scripts/martin-lint.py

anti_patterns.name: ouroboros
anti_patterns.rationale: Test that runs a test runner from within a test — infinite recursion risk

anti_patterns.name: unmarked_subprocess
anti_patterns.rationale: Subprocess calls spawn real processes — must be in e2e suite

anti_patterns.name: unmarked_notebook_execution
anti_patterns.rationale: Notebook execution is expensive — must be in e2e suite

anti_patterns.name: missing_subprocess_timeout
anti_patterns.rationale: Unbounded subprocess = potential hang, blocks entire suite

anti_patterns.name: tautological_assertion
anti_patterns.rationale: Assertion that always passes — tests nothing

anti_patterns.name: shared_state_write
anti_patterns.rationale: Test writes to shared data directory — use tmp_path instead

anti_patterns.name: duplicate_test_name
anti_patterns.rationale: Two tests with the same name — one silently overwrites the other

anti_patterns.name: unmarked_network_call
anti_patterns.rationale: Test makes network calls — should use mocking or be in integration suite

anti_patterns.name: unmarked_database_access
anti_patterns.rationale: Test accesses a database — should use fixtures or be in integration suite

anti_patterns.name: sleep_in_test
anti_patterns.rationale: time.sleep() makes tests slow and non-deterministic — use mocking or freezegun

anti_patterns.name: hardcoded_path_in_test
anti_patterns.rationale: Hardcoded file path — use tmp_path or fixtures for portability

anti_patterns.name: bare_assert
anti_patterns.rationale: Bare assert on single variable — intent unclear, add comparison or message

anti_patterns.name: overly_broad_except
anti_patterns.rationale: Catching all exceptions hides real failures — catch specific exceptions

anti_patterns.name: float_assertion_without_approx
anti_patterns.rationale: Exact float comparison is fragile — use pytest.approx()

anti_patterns.name: test_too_long
anti_patterns.rationale: Test function exceeds 50 lines — consider splitting into focused tests

anti_patterns.name: test_missing_description
anti_patterns.rationale: Test name is too short or generic — describe expected behavior

anti_patterns.name: unmarked_disk_write
anti_patterns.rationale: Test writes to disk — use tmp_path fixture for test isolation

anti_patterns.name: environment_mutation
anti_patterns.rationale: Test mutates environment variables — use monkeypatch fixture for cleanup

anti_patterns.name: non_tmp_path_file_creation
anti_patterns.rationale: File creation outside tmp_path — use tmp_path fixture for automatic cleanup

anti_patterns.name: missing_test_prefix
anti_patterns.rationale: Test file does not start with 'test_' — pytest will not discover it

anti_patterns.name: unused_import_in_test
anti_patterns.rationale: Unused imports clutter test files — remove them

anti_patterns.name: import_side_effects
anti_patterns.rationale: Import triggers side effects (e.g., pipeline run on import) — refactor to guard with main block

anti_patterns.name: print_in_test
anti_patterns.rationale: print() in tests pollutes output — use logging or remove

anti_patterns.name: hardcoded_timestamp
anti_patterns.rationale: Hardcoded timestamp — tests may break over time, use relative dates or fixtures

anti_patterns.name: unmarked_io_operation
anti_patterns.rationale: Test performs I/O to external services — should use mocking or be in integration suite

anti_patterns.name: test_class_wrong_prefix
anti_patterns.rationale: Test class does not start with 'Test' — pytest will not discover it

anti_patterns.name: complex_test_logic
anti_patterns.rationale: Test has too many branches (if/else/for/while) — tests should be linear, not complex

anti_patterns.name: pytest_raises_too_broad
anti_patterns.rationale: Catching too-broad exception in pytest.raises — catch specific exception type

anti_patterns.name: missing_tmp_path_for_writes
anti_patterns.rationale: Test writes files but doesn't accept tmp_path fixture — risk of leftover files

anti_patterns.name: test_depends_on_cwd
anti_patterns.rationale: Test depends on current working directory — use tmp_path or explicit paths

markers.e2e: End-to-end tests — run with pytest -m e2e
markers.slow: Tests taking >10s — run with pytest -m slow
markers.integration: Tests requiring external resources (network, databases, filesystem)

categories.safety: Rules that prevent system damage (infinite loops, hangs, data loss)
categories.isolation: Rules that ensure tests don't depend on each other or external state
categories.assertions: Rules about assertion quality and correctness
categories.structure: Rules about test organization and discoverability
categories.performance: Rules that prevent tests from being unnecessarily slow
categories.reliability: Rules that prevent flaky or non-deterministic test behavior
